fields.title: Communicative English Skills I
fields.description: Develops foundational English communication skills for Ethiopian university freshmen, covering listening, speaking, reading, and writing for academic and everyday contexts.

fields.title: Communicative English Skills II
fields.description: Builds on Communicative English Skills I with advanced reading comprehension, academic writing, and oral presentation skills needed for university success.

fields.title: Basic Writing Skills
fields.description: Introduces the fundamentals of academic writing including paragraph structure, essay organization, grammar review, and citation techniques for university-level work.

fields.title: General Mathematics
fields.description: Covers core mathematical topics for Ethiopian university freshmen including sets, functions, matrices, sequences, and introductory calculus.

fields.title: Logic and Critical Thinking
fields.description: Introduces formal and informal logic, argument analysis, fallacies, and scientific reasoning to help students think critically across all disciplines.

fields.title: Civic and Ethical Education
fields.description: Examines citizenship, democratic values, human rights, constitutional principles, and ethical frameworks relevant to Ethiopian civic life.

fields.title: History of Ethiopia and the Horn
fields.description: Traces the political, cultural, and social history of Ethiopia and the Horn of Africa from ancient civilizations through the modern period.

fields.title: Geography of Ethiopia and the Horn
fields.description: Studies the physical and human geography of Ethiopia and the Horn region, including landforms, climate, population, resources, and regional development.

fields.title: Social Anthropology
fields.description: Explores human societies, cultures, kinship systems, religion, ethnicity, and social change with a focus on African and Ethiopian contexts.

fields.title: Introduction to Psychology
fields.description: Provides an overview of psychological science including biological bases of behavior, perception, learning, memory, personality, and mental health.

fields.title: Introduction to Philosophy
fields.description: Surveys major branches of philosophy including metaphysics, epistemology, ethics, aesthetics, and political philosophy through classic and contemporary texts.

fields.title: Information Technology
fields.description: Introduces computer hardware, software, operating systems, networking, internet fundamentals, and practical digital literacy skills for university students.

fields.title: General Physics
fields.description: Covers classical mechanics, waves, thermodynamics, electricity, and magnetism with problem-solving emphasis for science and engineering freshmen.

fields.title: General Chemistry
fields.description: Introduces atomic structure, chemical bonding, stoichiometry, states of matter, thermochemistry, and reaction kinetics for science and engineering students.

fields.title: General Biology
fields.description: Surveys the fundamental principles of life including cell biology, genetics, evolution, ecology, and the diversity of living organisms.

fields.title: Introduction to Academic English
fields.description: Overview of the course, importance of English in academic settings, and a diagnostic assessment of communication skills.

fields.title: Listening Skills: Understanding Main Ideas
fields.description: Strategies for identifying main ideas and supporting details while listening to lectures and conversations.

fields.title: Speaking Skills: Participating in Discussions
fields.description: How to express opinions, ask questions, and take turns in academic discussions and group work.

fields.title: Reading Comprehension: Skimming and Scanning
fields.description: Techniques for quickly finding information and understanding the gist of academic texts.

fields.title: Vocabulary Building for Academic Contexts
fields.description: Strategies for learning and using academic vocabulary from the Academic Word List in context.

fields.title: Advanced Reading Strategies
fields.description: Critical reading of academic articles: annotating, evaluating arguments, and synthesizing information from multiple sources.

fields.title: Oral Presentations: Structure and Delivery
fields.description: How to plan, structure, and deliver an effective academic presentation with confidence.

fields.title: Writing Summaries and Paraphrases
fields.description: Techniques for accurately summarizing and paraphrasing source material while avoiding plagiarism.

fields.title: Grammar Review: Complex Sentences
fields.description: Using subordinate clauses, relative clauses, and conjunctions to write complex academic sentences.

fields.title: The Writing Process: Planning to Drafting
fields.description: Step-by-step guide through brainstorming, outlining, drafting, and revising academic writing.

fields.title: Paragraph Structure and Unity
fields.description: Writing focused paragraphs with clear topic sentences, supporting details, and concluding sentences.

fields.title: Essay Organization: Introduction, Body, Conclusion
fields.description: How to structure a five-paragraph essay with a strong thesis statement and coherent body paragraphs.

fields.title: Punctuation and Grammar Essentials
fields.description: Review of common punctuation rules, subject-verb agreement, tense consistency, and sentence fragments.

fields.title: Citation and Referencing Basics
fields.description: Introduction to APA and MLA citation styles, in-text citations, and building a reference list.

fields.title: Sets, Relations, and Functions
fields.description: Set notation, Venn diagrams, types of functions, domain and range, and function composition.

fields.title: Matrices and Determinants
fields.description: Matrix operations, determinants, inverse matrices, and solving systems of linear equations.

fields.title: Sequences and Series
fields.description: Arithmetic and geometric sequences, sigma notation, and infinite series convergence basics.

fields.title: Introduction to Differential Calculus
fields.description: Limits, continuity, derivatives, differentiation rules, and applications to rate of change problems.

fields.title: Introduction to Integral Calculus
fields.description: Indefinite and definite integrals, the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus, and area under a curve.

fields.title: What Is Logic? Arguments and Premises
fields.description: Introduction to logical reasoning, identifying arguments, premises, conclusions, and the difference between valid and sound arguments.

fields.title: Deductive and Inductive Reasoning
fields.description: Distinguishing deductive validity from inductive strength, with syllogisms and real-world argument examples.

fields.title: Logical Fallacies
fields.description: Common informal fallacies including ad hominem, straw man, false dilemma, and slippery slope, with practice identifying them.

fields.title: Scientific Reasoning and Evidence
fields.description: How science generates and evaluates evidence, hypothesis testing, and distinguishing science from pseudoscience.

fields.title: Citizenship and Democratic Values
fields.description: Concepts of citizenship, rights and responsibilities, democratic participation, and civic engagement in Ethiopia.

fields.title: The Ethiopian Constitution and Human Rights
fields.description: Key provisions of the FDRE Constitution, the Bill of Rights, and Ethiopia's human rights obligations.

fields.title: Ethical Theories: An Overview
fields.description: Introduction to major ethical theories — consequentialism, deontology, and virtue ethics — and their application to moral dilemmas.

fields.title: Good Governance and Anti-Corruption
fields.description: Principles of good governance, transparency, accountability, and the social costs of corruption in Ethiopia.

fields.title: Ancient Civilizations: Aksum and D'mt
fields.description: The rise and fall of the Aksumite Empire, the D'mt kingdom, trade networks, and early Christianity in Ethiopia.

fields.title: Medieval Ethiopia: The Zagwe and Solomonic Dynasties
fields.description: Lalibela rock-hewn churches, restoration of the Solomonic dynasty, and medieval Ethiopian statehood.

fields.title: The Era of Princes and Ethiopian Reform
fields.description: Zemene Mesafint (Era of Princes), Emperor Tewodros II's reforms, and the reunification of Ethiopia.

fields.title: The Battle of Adwa and Ethiopian Sovereignty
fields.description: Emperor Menelik II, the 1896 Battle of Adwa, Ethiopia's victory against Italian colonialism, and its global significance.

fields.title: Modern Ethiopia: From Haile Selassie to the Present
fields.description: Emperor Haile Selassie's reign, the Derg regime, the EPRDF era, and key developments in contemporary Ethiopian politics.

fields.title: Physical Geography: Landforms and Rift Valley
fields.description: Ethiopian Highlands, the Great Rift Valley, major rivers (Nile, Awash, Omo), and diverse ecological zones.

fields.title: Climate and Agro-Ecological Zones
fields.description: Climatic patterns of Ethiopia and the Horn, rainfall distribution, drought vulnerability, and the main agro-ecological zones.

fields.title: Population and Ethnic Geography
fields.description: Population distribution, ethnic diversity, urbanization trends, and major cities of Ethiopia and the Horn.

fields.title: Natural Resources and Economic Geography
fields.description: Agriculture, minerals, water resources, energy potential, and regional economic development in the Horn of Africa.

fields.title: What Is Social Anthropology?
fields.description: Origins of anthropology, its sub-disciplines, fieldwork methods, and the concept of culture.

fields.title: Kinship, Family, and Marriage
fields.description: Systems of kinship and descent, family structures, and marriage practices across cultures with Ethiopian examples.

fields.title: Religion, Ritual, and Belief Systems
fields.description: Anthropological perspectives on religion, ritual practice, indigenous beliefs, and the role of religion in social cohesion.

fields.title: Ethnicity, Identity, and Social Change
fields.description: Ethnic identity formation, intergroup relations, modernization, and cultural change in African societies.

fields.title: What Is Psychology? History and Perspectives
fields.description: Definition, history, and major theoretical perspectives in psychology — psychoanalytic, behavioral, cognitive, and humanistic.

fields.title: Biological Bases of Behavior
fields.description: The nervous system, brain structures, neurotransmitters, and how biology shapes human thought and behavior.

fields.title: Learning, Memory, and Cognition
fields.description: Classical and operant conditioning, memory systems, forgetting, and cognitive processes that underlie human learning.

fields.title: Personality and Individual Differences
fields.description: Major theories of personality — trait, psychoanalytic, and social-cognitive — and how personality is measured.

fields.title: Mental Health: Stress, Disorders, and Wellbeing
fields.description: Understanding stress, common psychological disorders (anxiety, depression), and strategies for maintaining mental wellbeing.

fields.title: What Is Philosophy? Questions and Methods
fields.description: The nature of philosophical inquiry, core questions, and methods of philosophical analysis.

fields.title: Epistemology: Knowledge and Belief
fields.description: What is knowledge? Rationalism vs. empiricism, justified true belief, skepticism, and the limits of human knowledge.

fields.title: Metaphysics: Reality and Existence
fields.description: Core metaphysical questions — existence, identity, free will, mind-body problem, and the nature of reality.

fields.title: Ethics and Political Philosophy
fields.description: The good life, justice, social contract theory, and the philosophical foundations of political institutions.

fields.title: Introduction to Computers and Hardware
fields.description: Computer components, CPU, memory, storage devices, input/output peripherals, and how they work together.

fields.title: Operating Systems and Software
fields.description: Role of operating systems, file management, application software types, and open-source vs. proprietary software.

fields.title: Networking and the Internet
fields.description: Network types (LAN, WAN), TCP/IP basics, how the internet works, HTTP, and cybersecurity fundamentals.

fields.title: Productivity Tools: Word Processing and Spreadsheets
fields.description: Practical use of Microsoft Word, Excel, and presentation software for academic and professional tasks.

fields.title: Digital Literacy and Online Safety
fields.description: Responsible internet use, recognizing misinformation, data privacy, social media ethics, and safe digital habits.

fields.title: Mechanics: Motion, Forces, and Newton's Laws
fields.description: Kinematics, Newton's three laws of motion, friction, and solving force-body problems.

fields.title: Work, Energy, and Conservation Laws
fields.description: Work-energy theorem, kinetic and potential energy, conservation of energy, and power calculations.

fields.title: Waves and Sound
fields.description: Properties of waves, wave equation, sound waves, the Doppler effect, and resonance phenomena.

fields.title: Electricity and Magnetism
fields.description: Electric charge, Coulomb's law, electric circuits (Ohm's law, series/parallel), and magnetic fields.

fields.title: Thermodynamics: Heat and Temperature
fields.description: Temperature scales, heat transfer, the laws of thermodynamics, and entropy.

fields.title: Atomic Structure and the Periodic Table
fields.description: Atomic theory, subatomic particles, electron configuration, and periodic trends.

fields.title: Chemical Bonding: Ionic and Covalent Bonds
fields.description: Types of chemical bonds, Lewis structures, molecular geometry (VSEPR theory), and polarity.

fields.title: Stoichiometry and Chemical Equations
fields.description: Balancing equations, molar mass, mole concept, limiting reagents, and percent yield calculations.

fields.title: States of Matter and Solutions
fields.description: Gas laws, liquid and solid properties, concentration units, solubility, and colligative properties.

fields.title: Thermochemistry and Reaction Kinetics
fields.description: Enthalpy, Hess's law, activation energy, and factors that affect the rate of chemical reactions.

fields.title: Cell Biology: The Basic Unit of Life
fields.description: Prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells, cell organelles, cell membrane structure, and transport mechanisms.

fields.title: Genetics: DNA, Heredity, and Inheritance
fields.description: DNA structure, replication, transcription, translation, Mendel's laws, and patterns of inheritance.

fields.title: Evolution and Natural Selection
fields.description: Darwin's theory of evolution, evidence for evolution, natural selection, adaptation, and speciation.

fields.title: Ecology: Organisms and Their Environment
fields.description: Ecosystems, food chains, energy flow, biogeochemical cycles, biodiversity, and human impacts on ecosystems.

fields.title: Diversity of Life: Classification and Major Groups
fields.description: Biological classification (taxonomy), the five/six kingdoms, and key characteristics of major groups from bacteria to mammals.
